sections.heading: Definition
sections.body:
The `in` operator in MongoDB Search performs a search for an array of BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) `number`,
`date`, `boolean`, `objectId`, `uuid`, or `string` values at the given
path and returns documents where the value of the field equals any value
in the specified array. If the field holds an array, then the
`in` operator selects the documents whose field holds an array that
contains at least one element that matches any value in the specified
array.

sections.heading: Syntax
Fields
sections.body:
| Field | Type                                             | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Necessity |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- |
| path  | string or array of strings                       | Indexed field or fields to search. You can also specify a wildcard path to search.To search for string values in a field, you must index the field as the MongoDB Search token type.                                                                                                                     | Required  |
| score | object                                           | Score to assign to matching search term results. Use one of the following options to modify the score:boost: multiply the result score by the given number.constant: replace the result score with the given number.function: replace the result score using the function expression.                    | Optional  |
| value | boolean, objectId, number, date, uuid, or string | Value or values to search. Value can be either a single value or an array of values of only one of the supported BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) types and can't be a mix of different types.To search for string values in a field, you must index the field as the MongoDB Search token type. | Required  |

sections.heading: Examples
sections.body:
The following examples use the `in` operator to query collections in
the [sample\_analytics.customers](sample-analytics) collection. If
you load the sample data on your cluster and [create a MongoDB Search index](ref-create-index) a MongoDB Search index named `default` that uses
[dynamic mappings](static-dynamic-mappings) on the collection, you
can run the following queries against the collections.
